approach: matches every prior layout-node CR's leash; the requester is in-session for this one, so a spec-gate self-assert lands in the async review queue rather than running unattended

purely additive to the schema: the tabs form joins root and panes as a third mutually-exclusive top-level shape, so every frozen layout scenario pinning the single-tree form stays true and no Clearance floor fires

the geometry seam widens by a NEW OPTIONAL member rather than changing describeRegion, so an adapter implementing only the region read still satisfies the contract; package is pre-1.0 regardless, so the Compatibility ceiling is not approached

the issue's own proposed mechanism was falsified at intake: injecting the workspace into the tab label cannot be parsed back, since a workspace label containing the separator is ambiguous under any split rule. The label prefix is kept for what it is actually good at (a human reading a status bar) and an opaque tmux window user option carries the grouping capture reads

mux/'s tab-naming non-goal is reconciled rather than contradicted: its premise holds only for a new workspace's ROOT tab, which herdr cannot name at birth; every subsequent tab names at birth on both backends
no plugin registry present - SDD default production chain for every touched artifact-type

every backend claim probed against live herdr 0.7.4 and tmux 3.6b rather than inferred from docs, matching this project's standing bar; the tmux window-option restart question was spiked rather than assumed

why.floor: Clearance - FIRED and pre-authorized by the requester at intake, the one floor this CR trips. This CORRECTS the run-start leash, which predicted 'purely additive ... no Clearance floor fires' before the frozen suite was read (combat log seq:1, explore-finding). layout.feature's frozen 'exactly one of root and panes' carries the Examples row 'neither root nor panes -> exits 1'; a tabs-only template declares neither, so the frozen contract refused the exact thing this CR adds. The outline is rewritten to the three-form schema - a narrowing however the net reads, since the replacement row no longer refuses a tabs-only template. Verified structurally rather than by line diff: gherkin-cli diff --base main = 31 added / 0 modified / 1 removed, the removal being exactly that outline and nothing else; the pure-move exemption is not claimed and does not apply. The rewrite is otherwise STRICTER - both original rows still exit 1, and it gains 'root and tabs' and 'panes and tabs'. The cheaper alternative (overload root to accept a tab list, so 'declares root' stays true and the outline is untouched) was offered to the requester and declined; the cold judge independently confirmed declining was right, since it would inferstructure from object-vs-array and violate the schema's own stated rule that type is an explicit discriminant because an inferred union produces terrible errors on a typo. mux.feature is the opposite class - 5 added / 0 modified / 0 removed, addOnly true, so its freeze self-clears with no re-open. Compatibility: no production code changed at this gate, so no semver class to exceed; both seam additions are NEW OPTIONAL members, so an adapter implementing neither still satisfies the contract, and the package is pre-1.0 regardless.
why.blast: high - two behavioral nodes, both gaining surface, plus a design-doc correction. layout/ gains the tabs form, the multi-tab walk, the two-carrier workspace grouping, the manifest tab field and workspace-wide capture (31 scenarios); mux/ gains the opaque group id on the open contract (5 scenarios) and reverses its own tab-naming non-goal. Scope held to issue #14: the cold judge checked all 5 of the issue's open questions were answered or explicitly declined, and found none silently dropped and nothing beyond them. Three settled decisions were deliberately NOT reopened despite being adjacent and tempting: apply still never steals focus (a template naming the tab to focus is the obvious next field and was declined, not deferred - a multi-tab apply lands more spaces at once, which makes stealing focus worse rather than more justified); save's default subject stays the caller's region, with --workspace opting in, so every region-scoped frozen save scenario stays literally true; and the manifest's workspace stays null on tmux, because the group tag is cyber-mux's own bookkeeping rather than a tier the backend gained - reporting it would be the confident lie the absent-rather-than-false convention exists to prevent.
why.novelty: high - the issue's own proposed mechanism is FALSIFIED, and the fix is not a patch on it. #14 proposes carrying the workspace grouping in the tab label as '<workspace> - <tab>'. That label cannot be parsed back: a window named 'acme - beta - main' reads as workspace 'acme' / tab 'beta - main' exactly as well as workspace 'acme - beta' / tab 'main', and no split rule resolves it - each merely picks which legal label to silently mis-group. Established by creating that window on tmux 3.6b, not by reasoning. The resolution splits the job in two rather than choosing a side: the label prefix is kept for what it is genuinely good at (a human reading a status bar, which is the issue's actual intent) and an opaque tmux window user option carries what capture reads, verified working including a server-side filter and survival of a window rename. That also DISSOLVES two of the issue's five open questions rather than answering them - shortening and collision only exist if the label is load-bearing, and it is not. A second premise fell: mux/'s tab-naming non-goal generalizes from a real constraint that binds only herdr's ROOT tab (workspace create labels it 1 with no flag); every later tab names at birth on both backends, which the frozen --label outline already specifies - so the reversal costs one tab rename, not a capability neither backend has. Third: docs/design/layout-templates.md asserts herdr's layout.export takes a tab_id as if reachable, but herdr layout is NOT a CLI verb in 0.7.4 (socket-API-only) while the adapter speaks the CLI by design - so the road it names is closed; corrected here, and the pane-per-tab indirection is not a downgrade, since an unfocused tab in another workspace reports live geometry and nothing needs focusing first. The tmux window-option restart worry was spiked rather than assumed and evaporated: a server restart drops the option but destroys every window with it, so there are no panes left to capture; the residual exposure is resurrect-style tooling, a stated limit.
why.confidence: high - cold sdd-spec-judge (fresh context, independent) 3-lens {oracle,builder,architect} all PASS, ALIGNED true, 36/36 new scenarios passing, zero failing, no blocker, no open markers; mechanical check-suite + check-spec-state green, both suites parse (layout 97 scenarios / 0 errors, mux 82 / 0). It re-derived rather than trusted: it re-ran both edit classifications structurally and parsed the JSON rather than the summary footer; it confirmed the rewritten outline is a strict superset and that no old-valid template becomes newly invalid; it verified the frozen tmux-workspace-null scenario is BYTE-IDENTICAL to main and that the tabs-aware claim rides a new sibling rather than a rewrite; it grepped the adapters for the group-id vocabulary and found none, confirming the seam scenarios are new surface rather than reverse-engineered from code; it ran the miss test over all 36 and found none inert; and it checked pairwise consistency across all 97 layout + 82 mux scenarios against the full text, finding no contradiction. Every empirical backend claim was independently REPRODUCED live - the label ambiguity, the @cm_ws tag surviving a rename and dying with its window, and herdr returning live geometry for an unfocused tab in an unfocused workspace - against the same binaries. It also caught a real defect in the producer's own record: the run-start leash's stale 'no Clearance fires' claim, corrected above rather than left standing. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash (by derived) - ratify or kick back.

title: worktree add --layout leaves a tabs template's windows ungrouped on a backend with no workspace tier
note: Contradicts a completion claim this CR's own spec makes. layout/README's tabs use case states the grouping is carried TWICE where the backend lacks a workspace tier - the label prefix for a human, the opaque group id for a machine - and does not scope that claim to one route. It holds for 'open --layout' and does NOT hold for 'worktree add --layout': that route passes no group id, so on tmux the windows carry the prefix (a human sees the group) but no tag (capture does not). 'save --workspace' then reports each window as the workspace-of-one it honestly is, so a 3-tab workspace built by 'worktree add --layout' round-trips as 1 tab. Same flag, same template, same backend, different grouping by route - the shape of leak this node's own conventions exist to prevent. No frozen scenario is violated: the walk's grouping is specified without naming a route, and 'an untagged region captures as a single-tab workspace' is the frozen, correct answer for a window nobody grouped. So the suite is consistent and the PROSE over-claims. Found by the impl-producer, which passed no group id rather than tag tabs 2..N and leave the workspace's own first tab out - a partial group is worse than none, since capture would then confidently round-trip 3 tabs as 2. herdr is unaffected: it has a real tier and ignores the tag. Root cause is structural rather than an oversight: the seam carries a group id only AT an open, and on this route the first tab's region is opened by the worktree verbs before the walk runs, so grouping it requires the worktree open itself to carry the id (worktree-session.ts's options + addAndOpenWorktree, with cli.ts minting the id before the open and handing the same one to the walk). That is a spec question, not just plumbing: herdr's worktree verbs already refuse env at birth under a frozen scenario, so what they accept is not assumable and must be established live before it is specified.

why.floor: none at the impl gate. The CR's one floor (Clearance, for the layout outline rewrite) fired and was pre-authorized at the spec gate, and nothing at deliver widened it: the layout edit is still exactly 34 added / 0 modified / 1 removed, the removal being that one outline and nothing else, and mux is still addOnly. Re-verified structurally by the cold judge against every scenario's change field rather than the summary counts. No frozen scenario was narrowed, rewritten or deleted at deliver. Every defect - including three that would have SHIPPED - was fixed in code, never by editing a scenario to fit the implementation. Compatibility: the package is 0.0.0/pre-1.0 and the changeset is minor; SessionAdapter gains two REQUIRED members (rename, group) and OpenedPane a required tab, which is breaking for a third-party adapter author, but there is none and the seam is the project's own. So the semver class does not exceed the change-class ceiling.
why.blast: high - two behavioral nodes, both gaining surface, plus a design-doc correction. 47 new scenarios (34 layout, 13 mux), ALL bound and passing, with ZERO unbound debt added: the 37 layout and 7 mux that remain unbound are the untouched pre-existing baseline. 512 tests, pnpm verify green 6/6. The seam grew more than the spec gate anticipated - rename, group, OpenedPane.tab and the @cm_tab option were all specified DURING deliver, because building falsified the spec gate's implicit assumption that the existing seam could express what layout/ already required. Each addition was additive to a frozen suite, so each self-cleared with no re-open and no floor. Three settled decisions stayed closed under pressure: apply still never steals focus, save's bare default is unchanged (so every region-scoped frozen save scenario stays literally true), and the manifest's workspace stays null on tmux.
why.novelty: high - the CR's real finding is a PATTERN, not any one bug, and it is the conductor's own. THREE would-have-shipped defects traced to one habit: a behavior specified against the route being built, worded as a property of the template, bound on ONE route, and silently false on the other. All three were green in CI and wrong on a real backend. (1) grouping was an open option only, so worktree add --layout could not group a region the worktree verbs had already opened, and save --workspace round-tripped a 3-tab workspace as 1. (2) a composed display name DESTROYS the tab's own name - tmux has one name field - so capture read 'pool - editor' back as the label and re-applying gave 'pool - pool - editor', making the frozen round-trip scenario FALSE on tmux. (3) the worktree route never renamed its first tab, though the scenario says 'when it is applied on tmux' and names no route. Root cause of all three: the rule 'the display label is a human's to read, an opaque option carries what a machine reads back' was established and then applied to the WORKSPACE ONLY. Instance (1) was surfaced by the REQUESTER rejecting the conductor's premise - the conductor had reported a self-imposed design limit as a fact about the backends and proposed scoping the spec's prose down to match it; @cm_ws is an ordinary window option settable on any window at any time. The fix is structural rather than three patches: the first-tab naming and grouping now live in walkTabs, the one engine both routes converge on, so a route can no longer forget. The cold judge specifically hunted a fourth instance and found none, giving that reason.
why.confidence: high - cold sdd-impl-judge (fresh context, independent) IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true, 47/47 new scenarios pass, 0 fail, no blocker, no absorption findings. It ran rather than read: it stood up a throwaway tmux server and LIVE-REPRODUCED all three fixes end to end (all three windows carrying the same @cm_ws including the first; the first window renamed at birth; a live save --workspace returning unprefixed labels and re-applying to a single prefix rather than a compounded one). It wrote and reverted its OWN mutations rather than confirming the producer's - skipping group() for tab 0, disabling the first-tab rename, and reversing tmux's ownName-over-windowName priority - each killed by the tests that should kill it, including the acme-beta-main ambiguity scenario. It re-ran both edit classifications structurally and checked every scenario's change field rather than the summary. It verified commit 719f4ad's three-bugs claim against its actual diff rather than its message, the exact failure this project has shipped before. It hunted the fourth false-bind systematically and found none. Earlier rounds caught two false-greens that are worth the record: a tabs-default test bound on tmux SURVIVED mutating its own subject, because tmux collapses workspace and tab onto the same new-window so no assertion over that argv can discriminate the tiers - moved to herdr; and a capture test asserted the label came back as 'acme - beta - main', the bug's own twin masquerading as coverage. The judge also caught a real miss: the changeset was written but UNTRACKED, staged and committed as 4ecd471. Self-asserted within the auto-spec leash (by derived) - ratify or kick back.

title: RESOLVED IN-CR, DO NOT FILE: the seq:3 worktree-grouping follow-up
note: Supersedes the seq:3 followup, which is append-only and therefore stands as written. That entry recorded 'worktree add --layout leaves a tabs template's windows ungrouped on a backend with no workspace tier' as blocking. It was FIXED inside this CR, not deferred: grouping became a verb over an already-open space, the worktree route groups its own first tab, and the frozen scenario 'a tabs template groups the same way whichever verb opened the workspace' now binds and passes, live-reproduced by the cold impl-judge on a real tmux server (all three windows carrying the same @cm_ws, the first included). Recorded because a drain re-derives what is outstanding by deduping against the forge, and seq:3 carries no filed-state by design - so without this line a later drain would file an issue for a bug that no longer exists. Nothing to file.

title: A workspace capture whose live tabs share a label writes a template its own validator rejects
note: Genuine gap, surfaced by the capture impl-producer and deliberately not invented around. 'two tabs sharing a label are refused' is a frozen VALIDATION rule, but no capture scenario says what --workspace does when two LIVE tabs happen to share a label. Pane-label collisions have an explicit rule ('a label two panes share is dropped from both, with a warning'); tab labels have no analogue. So the literal contract was implemented - 'a captured tab keeps the label its tab carries' - and a capture of such a workspace writes a file that fails its own validate. The drop-both rule would have been the producer's invention rather than the spec's, which is why it stopped. The hole shows from the other side too: 'a captured template passes validate' is scoped to a REGION capture and has no --workspace counterpart. Fixing it is a spec decision (drop both and warn, mirroring panes? suffix? refuse?), not a code choice.

title: The tmux group tag's survival of a window rename is pinned by mechanism, not by an assertion
note: The spec states the tmux window option survives a window rename, and it does - verified live against tmux 3.6b at intake and again by the cold spec-judge. But no unit test asserts it: the adapters are tested through a mocked Exec, which cannot observe a real rename. The property is pinned by the CHOICE of mechanism (an @-prefixed window user option) rather than by a check, so a future change to a mechanism that does not survive renaming would pass every test. The repo has an integration tier (session.tmux.integration.test.ts) where a real rename-survival check belongs. Related, and out of scope for the same reason: the option does not survive a tmux server restart, but neither does any window, so nothing is lost; the residual exposure is resurrect-style tooling restoring windows without their user options, which would silently degrade a workspace to N workspaces-of-one.

title: DISSOLVED, DO NOT FILE: the seq:6 duplicate-tab-label capture follow-up
note: Supersedes seq:6, which is append-only and stands as written. That entry recorded that a --workspace capture whose live tabs share a label writes a template its own validator rejects, and framed the open question as which collision rule to adopt (drop both and warn, mirroring panes? suffix? refuse?). The premise is gone: the requester dismantled label uniqueness itself, so a duplicate label is now LEGAL at both tiers and a capture carrying one validates cleanly. There is no collision rule to choose, because there is no collision. Verified by driving captureLayout on two panes both labeled worker: the template carries both labels, warnings is empty, and validateLayout returns no errors. Recorded because a drain re-derives outstanding work by deduping against the forge, and seq:6 carries no filed-state by design - without this line a later drain would file an issue for a question that no longer exists. Nothing to file. The real successor is the pane-lookup verb, filed as issue #31.

title: CLOSED BY REQUESTER, DO NOT FILE: the seq:7 rename-survival test-depth follow-up
note: Supersedes seq:7, which is append-only and stands as written. That entry proposed an integration-tier test pinning that the tmux group tag survives a window rename, since the mocked Exec cannot observe one. The requester closed it directly: it is an external dependency's behavior, it does not need a suite, and it should be described in the spec instead. Done - the mux node's prose now states the tag's full lifetime: it survives a rename (the reason it, and not the name, carries the grouping), it dies with the tmux server but so does every window it could have tagged (so nothing is lost), and the one real exposure is session-restoring tooling bringing windows back without their user options, which reads as N workspaces-of-one. That last is a stated limit of an external tool rather than a defect here, and not something the seam can defend against. Nothing to file.
